pattern: A candidate string can only repeat k times if the candidate formed by dropping its last character also repeats k times, so growing candidates one character at a time in increasing length order (breadth-first) and discarding any that fail lets the search prune itself instead of enumerating every possible string.
hints: Only letters that individually appear at least k times in s can ever start a valid candidate, since even a length-1 candidate needs k copies.

Grow candidates breadth-first, one appended character at a time, starting from those qualifying single letters; check whether a candidate repeated k times is a subsequence of s using the classic two-pointer subsequence test.

Only extend a candidate that itself already passed the repeated-subsequence check (if a candidate fails, every longer string built from it fails too), and keep the longest -- lexicographically largest on ties -- candidate seen so far.

edge_inputs.description: a single repeated letter with no other characters
a short pattern repeated exactly k times with nothing else
a larger k with just enough repetitions of a single letter
a two-letter alternating pattern where the whole string is one exact repetition
three equally-frequent letters where the full three-letter pattern beats any single letter
no letter appears often enough, so no repeated subsequence exists at all
two letters each meeting the threshold, with moderate length to exercise the growth search

stress.description: random short strings over a small alphabet with random k respecting the length bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(2, 50)
    max_n = min(2000, 8 * k - 1)
    n = max(2, min(size, max_n))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghij'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s, k]

stress.description: near the maximum string length, using a large k and a tiny alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = 250
    max_n = min(2000, 8 * k - 1)
    n = max(2, min(size, max_n))
    alphabet = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s, k]

complexity.time: bounded by the small search space implied by n < 8k
